rect.data-narration: The Waste Hierarchy: A guiding framework for waste minimization strategies.

Reduce (Prevention): The most effective method. It involves designing products and processes to minimize the amount of waste created. Examples include using digital documents instead of paper, buying products with less packaging, and designing longer-lasting goods.
Reuse: Using existing products, materials, or resources to minimize waste. Examples include buying second-hand items, using reusable bags and containers, and sharing resources.
Recycle/Recover: Converting waste materials into new products or resources. Examples include recycling paper, plastic, and glass, and recovering materials from waste.
Recovery: Collecting and processing non-recyclable materials to produce energy or raw materials. Examples include biogas production and waste-to-energy plants.

Dispose: The least preferred method. It involves sending waste to landfills or incinerators. Examples include sending hazardous waste to specialized facilities and disposing of non-recyclable materials in landfills.
